questions.text: Why is the story of Yusuf (AS) called the 'Best of Stories'?
questions.answer: Because of its detailed narrative, emotional depth, lessons on patience, and the demonstration of how Allah's plan prevails over human plotting.

questions.text: What was the dream Yusuf (AS) saw as a child?
questions.answer: He saw eleven stars, the sun, and the moon prostrating to him.

questions.text: What advice did Ya'qub (AS) give Yusuf after hearing his dream?
questions.answer: He told him not to relate his vision to his brothers, lest they plot against him out of jealousy.

questions.text: How did the brothers of Yusuf justify their plot against him?
questions.answer: They claimed that Yusuf and his brother were more beloved to their father than they were, though they were a strong group.

questions.text: What evidence did the brothers bring to their father to prove Yusuf's death?
questions.answer: They brought his shirt stained with false blood, claiming a wolf had eaten him.

questions.text: What was the reaction of Ya'qub (AS) to the news of Yusuf's death?
questions.answer: He said, 'Patience is most fitting' (Sabrun Jameel) and suspected their story was fabricated.

questions.text: Who bought Yusuf (AS) after he was rescued from the well?
questions.answer: The Aziz (high official) of Egypt.

questions.text: How did Yusuf (AS) respond to the temptation of the Aziz's wife?
questions.answer: He sought refuge in Allah, remaining loyal to his master and his faith.

questions.text: How was Yusuf's innocence initially suggested when the Aziz's wife accused him?
questions.answer: A witness noted that if his shirt was torn from the back, she was lying and he was truthful.

questions.text: Why did Yusuf (AS) choose prison over the alternative?
questions.answer: He preferred prison over committing the sin the women were calling him toward.

questions.text: What did Yusuf (AS) do first when the two prisoners asked him to interpret their dreams?
questions.answer: He invited them to the worship of Allah alone before providing the interpretation.

questions.text: What was the King's dream that no one else could interpret?
questions.answer: Seven fat cows being eaten by seven lean ones, and seven green ears of grain and others dry.

questions.text: What was Yusuf's interpretation of the King's dream?
questions.answer: Seven years of abundance followed by seven years of severe famine.

questions.text: Why did Yusuf (AS) refuse to leave prison when the King's messenger arrived?
questions.answer: He wanted his name cleared and the truth about the women's incident to be officially acknowledged first.

questions.text: What position did Yusuf (AS) ask for after being cleared?
questions.answer: To be in charge of the storehouses of the land (finance/agriculture minister).

questions.text: How did Yusuf (AS) ensure his brothers would bring Benjamin to Egypt?
questions.answer: He returned their goods/money in their bags so they would have an incentive to return.

questions.text: What did the brothers say when the stolen cup was found in Benjamin's bag?
questions.answer: They said if he stole, a brother of his (Yusuf) had also stolen before, still showing their old bias.

questions.text: How was Ya'qub's (AS) eyesight restored?
questions.answer: He cast Yusuf's shirt over his face, and his vision returned by Allah's leave.

questions.text: What was the first thing Yusuf (AS) said to his brothers after revealing his identity?
questions.answer: He told them there is no blame upon them that day and asked Allah to forgive them.

questions.text: What is the core lesson of the Surah regarding the 'Command of Allah'?
questions.answer: Allah is predominant over His affairs, but most of the people do not know.
